title: Experiential Learning in Data Science
notes: Hybrid capstone-style course with industry, non-profit, and institutional project sponsors; includes professional skills training in teamwork, leadership, and communication.

title: Special Topics in Vertically Integrated Projects
notes: Course created to allow students to earn credit while doing academic work; model planned for expansion campus-wide.

title: Matrix Algebra with Numerical Applications
notes: Coordinated multiple sections per semester; developed and maintained syllabus, assignments, and Jupyter-based course materials.

title: Methods for Parallel Programming
notes: Course focused on shared-memory, accelerator, and distributed parallel programming; extensive use of HPC systems.

title: Methods in Computational Modeling
notes: Redesigned course to emphasize project-based work tied to students’ own graduate research; focus on robust scientific software practices.

title: Computational Modeling Using the MFEM Library
notes: Graduate special topics course using the MFEM library; students define learning goals collaboratively with instructors.

title: Generative AI
notes: Special topics course focused on generative AI methods and applications.

title: Communications, Teamwork, Ethics, and Leadership for Multidisciplinary Research Teams
notes: Professional skills course integrating NSF CyberAmbassador materials, Responsible Conduct of Research, and NIH Entering Mentoring training.

title: Learning in the Digital Age: Academic, Personal, and Professional Considerations
notes: Fully online course focused on professional skills and digital tools for new students.

title: Designing and Building Applications for Extreme Scale Systems
notes: Multi-university course led by University of Illinois; taught using a flipped-classroom model.

title: Study of Scientific Measurement Using Digital Images and Video
notes: Engineering Honors research seminar focused on image and video analysis in cross-disciplinary science.

title: Translation of Programming Languages (Compilers)
notes: Senior-level technical elective; included x86 assembly and multi-core optimization concepts.

title: Engineering Teamwork Seminar
notes: Weekend seminar on communication and interpersonal skills for engineers; sponsored by Tau Beta Pi.

title: Programming and Mathematical Concepts in MATLAB for Research Scientists
notes: Course for graduate students in statistics, psychology, zoology, telecommunications, and computer science.

title: Embedded System Design
notes: Assisted students with FPGA- and microcontroller-based embedded system design.
